title: Creature
excerpt: A creature is a rule-resolved being in the world that can have a stat block, occupy space, change state, and be affected by actions, damage, conditions, and other effects.
markdown:
# Creature

A creature is a being that the rules resolve as an active entity in the world. A creature can occupy space, move, take actions, gain conditions, suffer damage, and interact with effects that refer to creatures as targets, allies, enemies, or occupants of an area.

## Rules Use

### Active Entity Surface
- A creature is resolved through creature-facing rules rather than object-only handling
- A creature can have a stat block, current state, location, and rule-relevant traits
- A creature can be targeted by effects that specify a creature, a willing creature, an enemy, an ally, or another creature-based qualifier

### State Can Change
- A creature's current state can change through damage, healing, conditions, movement, transformation, control effects, or death
- Later rule resolution uses the creature's current state, not only its original one
- A creature can stop functioning as an active participant if another rule leaves it unconscious, incapacitated, dead, banished, dismissed, or otherwise unable to act

### Distinct From Objects
- A creature is not treated as an ordinary object while it is resolving as a creature
- Some effects can turn an object into a creature or leave behind an object or remains when creature-state ends
- When a rule distinguishes between creatures and objects, the current resolved state determines which handling applies

## Common Uses

- Determining whether an effect can target a creature at all
- Resolving movement, occupied space, and presence in an area
- Applying damage, healing, conditions, or saving throws to an active being
- Checking whether a creature is still an active participant in the current world-state
